calendarNotes:
Dallas ISD begins the 2026-2027 school year on August 11, 2026, and Spring Break runs March 15–19, 2027. The school year ends May 26, 2027. Like all large Texas districts, Dallas ISD starts in early August — well ahead of the national average — allowing the district to complete more instructional time before state STAAR testing in the spring. Families relocating from states that start after Labor Day should plan for mid-August back-to-school routines.

Dallas ISD shares the DFW metroplex with dozens of suburban districts including Plano, Frisco, Garland, Richardson, Irving, and Arlington — none of which are part of Dallas ISD. Their Spring Break dates typically align within a day or two of Dallas ISD's, but this is not guaranteed every year. In some years Plano ISD and Frisco ISD may break a day earlier or later, which matters for childcare and family travel planning. Always verify the calendar of each specific district rather than assuming DFW-wide alignment.

Dallas ISD operates one of the largest magnet school networks in Texas, spanning STEM, bilingual, arts, and International Baccalaureate programs. The district's school choice enrollment process opens in January for the following school year — months before most families start thinking about school options. Families interested in specialty programs should attend a choice expo in the fall and submit applications by the January deadline. Popular programs like the STEM Early College High School and the School of Science and Engineering fill quickly.

Dallas summers are extreme — temperatures regularly exceed 105°F in July and August. Back-to-school shopping is most practical in late July, before the heat peaks and before school-supply aisles run low on popular items. The week before school starts (around August 4–8) is typically the busiest and hottest week for shopping near Dallas ISD schools.
